plot_context_observations: Action/espionage and franchise set-pieces (multiple Mission: Impossible entries; Jack Ryan; Tom Cruise recurring)
Science-fiction focus on digital/virtual worlds and AI (Tron, Tron: Ares, Tron: Legacy; The Terminator; Dune; Mickey 17)
Revenge and vigilantism arcs (Sisu: Road to Revenge; Lady Snowblood; Monkey Man; One Battle After Another)
War, legal aftermath and historical dramas (Nuremberg; Band of Brothers; The American Revolution; Chief of War)
Post-apocalyptic and survival scenarios (Fallout; The Last of Us; 28 Days Later; Prospect)
Family, grief and intimate character drama (Hamnet; Hereditary; If I Had Legs I'd Kick You; Titanic)
Crime, police and institutional-corruption stories (End of Watch; U-571; Blue Lights; Task)
Dark/genre-blending horror and mystery (The Black Phone; Hereditary; Saw; Weapons)
Musical/album consumption alongside films (Pink Floyd album tracks and concert/album content present)
history_summary.executive_summary: Recent viewing skews toward high-energy action, science fiction (including virtual/digital worlds), and prestige drama — a mix of blockbuster/franchise binges (Mission: Impossible, Lord of the Rings, Tron) and auteur-driven releases (Nolan, Bong, Chloé Zhao, Paul Thomas Anderson). The history also shows interest in revenge narratives, war/historical dramas, survival/post-apocalyptic stories, and occasional comedy/rom-com and music listening (Pink Floyd, Santana).
source_gaps: Cinematographer credits are absent or empty for nearly all items in the provided history — cannot report on DP preferences.

Some entries lack metadata (e.g., missing summary, cast or director for items like 'Warfare', 'Beauty and the Beast', 'The Man in the High Castle', 'The Rehearsal'); conclusions drawn only from available fields.
Cinematographer metadata was not present in the supplied Plex items.

recommendations.executive_summary: Prioritize high-energy action and sci-fi (virtual/digital worlds, AI) plus revenge/post‑apocalyptic titles and prestige drama — matching recent franchise binges (Tron, Mission: Impossible, LOTR), survival narratives, and crime/thriller interest. On-server picks are available in the provided Plex candidates and skew 75% movies / 25% shows; off-server picks fill gaps for dystopian/post‑apoc, revenge, and auteur-driven sci‑fi.

on_server_recommendations.title: Back to the Future
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Classic time‑travel sci‑fi that aligns with interest in virtual/digital-world concepts and genre‑blend sci‑fi.

on_server_recommendations.title: Edge of Tomorrow
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Action-forward sci‑fi with repetitive virtual‑simulation structure and high-energy set pieces.

on_server_recommendations.title: Minority Report
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Tech-driven crime thriller that matches interest in AI/virtual surveillance and crime/institutional narratives.

on_server_recommendations.title: Django Unchained
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Revenge-driven, auteur-directed violent drama — fits the user's interest in revenge narratives and prestige directors.

on_server_recommendations.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Relentless post‑apocalyptic action and survival focus that echoes the user's post‑apoc viewing (Fallout, The Last of Us).

on_server_recommendations.title: John Wick
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Stylized, high‑octane action with revenge at its core — directly aligns with action and revenge preferences.

on_server_recommendations.title: Westworld
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Series about AI, virtual realities and moral ambiguity — closely matches digital/virtual world interests and prestige TV appetite.
supporting_signals: viewer_profile: Sci‑Fi & Virtual Worlds; Prestige auteurs

on_server_recommendations.title: The Peripheral
on_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Near‑future tech thriller that explores virtual interfaces and surveillance — complements Tron/AI viewing patterns.

off_server_recommendations.title: Children of Men
off_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Dystopian, politically charged survival drama matching interest in post‑apocalyptic and historical/war stakes.

off_server_recommendations.title: Snowpiercer
off_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Class-driven post‑apocalyptic action with survival and revenge elements — fits post‑apoc and institutional-conflict interests.
off_server_recommendations.lookup_hint: Acquire: Bong Joon‑ho (dir); 2013; post‑apoc class allegory

off_server_recommendations.title: Oldboy
off_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Intense revenge thriller with genre‑blending violence and psychological twists — aligns with revenge-driven preferences.
supporting_signals: viewer_profile: Revenge-driven narratives; Dark genre interest

off_server_recommendations.title: District 9
off_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Sci‑fi allegory combining social conflict with high‑concept tech themes — complements interest in sociopolitical sci‑fi.
off_server_recommendations.lookup_hint: Acquire: Neill Blomkamp (dir); 2009; sci‑fi social allegory

off_server_recommendations.title: The Road
off_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Bleak, intimate survival and grief story that dovetails with the user's interest in family/grief dramas within post‑apoc settings.
off_server_recommendations.lookup_hint: Acquire: Cormac McCarthy adaptation; 2009; bleak survival drama

off_server_recommendations.title: Moon
off_server_recommendations.why_it_matches: Isolated, introspective sci‑fi exploring identity and AI — echoes the user's AI/virtual world and auteur tastes.
supporting_signals: viewer_profile: Sci‑Fi & Virtual Worlds; Prestige auteurs
off_server_recommendations.lookup_hint: Acquire: Duncan Jones (dir); 2009; solitary AI identity drama
